matches.x: CHAPTER II. An exhortation—The judgment of the Phite iad and Ammon— Ethiopia and ssyria, Ca yourselves together, yea, gather together, O nation not desired ;

We will close with the word of the Lord through Zephaniah (Ch.,ii. v. 1—3;)' — “ Gather yourselves together ; yea, gather together, 0 nation not desired^ before the decree bring forth, before the day pass as the chaff, before the fierce anger of the Lord come upon you, before the day of the Lord’s anger come upon you. Seek ye the Lord, all ye meek of the earth which have wrought his judg- ment;…

traditionally interpreted by mainstream anti-Mormons as meaning that other than Yahweh no form of deity exists at all, including exalted men. This type of interpretation at first seems obvious, but after considering similar passages in other parts of scripture it is clear that this interpretation is incorrect. For example, Is. 47:8-10 depicts the city of Babylon as saying: Therefore hear now…
